title: Local OpenCode Run Session-Creation Failure RCA and Safe Recovery
pipeline_path: 2 -> 5 -> 7 -> 9, with mandatory evidence/backup and standalone plus attached-server smoke gates
pipeline_rationale: Stage 1 (plan creation) is already complete; the next path is Stage 2 (plan review) -> Stage 5 (execution) -> Stage 7 (validation) -> Stage 9 (closeout). No production source changes are planned, but shared session persistence and an upgrade create high operational risk. A full safety review and explicit validation gates are required; code-test stages are replaced by deterministic runtime diagnostics.
skipped_stages: Stage 3: run only if plan review identifies material ambiguity or disagreement (this Stage 2 review found none requiring re-review).
Stage 4 and Stage 4b: no application code or test suite is in scope; pre-change failure capture and post-change smoke tests are the required replacement gates.
Stage 6: no repository test command applies; runtime smoke tests and session persistence checks are mandatory instead.
Stage 8: skipped after Stage 7 because only three bookkeeping mismatches were found and reconciled; no contradiction or post-remediation failure remained.
test_command: n/a; use enumerated runtime smoke commands in plan.md
summary: Investigate the known 1.15.x session_message.seq failure pattern, protect the shared database, upgrade the canonical runtime, and separately validate standalone and headless-server attachment paths.
keyOutcomes: Evidence-based distinction between read-only session listing and session/message write health.
A non-destructive upgrade-first remediation path based on upstream issue and commit evidence.
Verified MiniMax standalone and attached-server results or a high-quality upstream escalation package.
risks: Database loss or unintended migration from destructive troubleshooting.
Coexisting binaries cause the wrong version to remain active.
MiniMax provider availability is mistaken for a session persistence defect.
dependencies: Operator approval for runtime upgrade and any PATH/install normalization.
A MiniMax model identifier returned by the upgraded OpenCode runtime.
Exclusive or coordinated access to the shared OpenCode database during backup and upgrade (stop OpenCode.exe Desktop processes first per plan.md P1.1).
deliverables: Verified database backup reference and compatibility assessment.
Standalone and attached-server validation results or an upstream issue draft.
validationCriteria: No database reset or manual schema patch is used in the primary remediation path.
Canonical CLI version is no longer 1.15.10 and contains the upstream seq fix or later stable equivalent.
Both standalone and attached-server smoke runs create/persist sessions successfully.
MiniMax is tested only with an enumerated available model identifier.

dry_run_targets: node:sqlite PRAGMA quick_check on live 14.36 GB DB returned [{"quick_check":"ok"}] in 68.857s
node:sqlite session_message schema probe confirmed seq INTEGER NOT NULL with no default
node:sqlite backup() API smoke on 1 MB synthetic DB returned 252 pages in 11 ms
opencode session list --max-count 1 --format json returned 1 session
opencode models listed opencode/minimax-m2.5, m2.7, m3 and opencode-go/minimax-m3

executor.routing_override: user-authorized; Z.AI quota exhausted for GLM-5.2 and GLM-5.1

execution_notes: P3.1 not needed (P2.1+P2.2 both passed). P3.2 bookkeeping completed. CLI 1.15.10 -> 1.18.1. No escalation required. Reconciled 2026-07-16: totalTasks 8->9, 19 spec checkboxes marked, dangling relatedTracks entry removed.

validation.stage8: skipped; bookkeeping findings reconciled and A+C threshold not met

documentation_closeout.stage9: waived; no public documentation surface or semantic setup/API change
